fields.description:
## Context

Agents across DSO workflows create bug tickets with free-form, inconsistent descriptions that make triage harder. The existing ticket CLI accepts any description, which is correct — blocking bug creation with strict format enforcement would cause agents to skip reporting entirely. But without guidance, bug reports lack structured expected/actual behavior, consistent titles, and useful logs. By providing a template with post-creation validation warnings (never gates), a lightweight create-bug skill, and using-dso guidance, agents get three chances to produce high-quality reports without any barrier to tracking issues.

## Success Criteria

- A shared bug report template exists at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md with required sections (Expected Behavior, Actual Behavior) and optional sections (Technical Environment, Action History and Workarounds, Logs, Additional Details)
- The Logs section includes guidance to truncate at 30K characters and use ticket comments for overflow
- The template specifies a structured title format: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] with the component hierarchy (Tool/Skill -> File/Path -> Logical Workflow)
- The ticket CLI auto-converts unicode arrow (U+2192) to ASCII -> in titles on create and edit, eliminating unicode compatibility issues with Jira
- Post-creation validation prints a warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the title does not match the [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] pattern, directing the agent to edit the title via the ticket edit command
- Post-creation validation prints a separate warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the description is missing Expected Behavior or Actual Behavior headers (case-insensitive check), directing the agent to edit the description via the ticket edit command
- Post-creation validation prints a warning to stderr (exit code 0) when the description exceeds 30K characters, directing the agent to move excess content to ticket comments
- Bugs are always created regardless of format compliance — warnings never block creation and exit code is always 0
- The title pattern warning is configurable via dso-config.conf (key: bug_report.title_warning_enabled, default: true) so projects with different title conventions can opt out
- A lightweight /dso:create-bug skill exists that references the template and the correct ticket command; the skill does not add --tags CLI_user by default (only the calling agent knows whether the user explicitly requested the bug)
- Using-dso guidance instructs agents to use the template when creating bugs

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Three-layer guidance with post-creation validation. A shared template (bug-report-template.md) defines the format with the Zero Inference rule (no root cause speculation, no hindsight bias, no forced debugging, no low-value tickets). Post-creation validation in the ticket CLI warns on missing title pattern, missing description headers, and oversized descriptions — warnings only to stderr, never blocking, exit code always 0. Unicode arrow auto-converted to ASCII on create/edit. Title warning configurable via dso-config.conf. A lightweight create-bug skill references the template. Using-dso guidance provides the third reminder.

## Scenario Analysis

- **30K log truncation advisory only — oversized tickets corrupt branch state** (high, runtime): A 200K+ character description could cause git operations on the tickets branch to time out. Addressed by adding a description size warning at 30K.
- **Post-creation validation not covered by tests** (high, deployment): If no tests cover the warning path, the validation layer could be dead code. Addressed by TDD requirement — RED tests before implementation.
- **Stderr warnings swallowed by $(...) pattern** (medium, runtime): Agents using OUTPUT=$(dso ticket create bug ...) capture stdout only, discarding stderr. Accepted risk — the other two layers (skill + using-dso) still apply.
- **Shim enforcement blocks create-bug skill** (medium, deployment): check-shim-refs.sh will block commits if the skill references plugin scripts directly. Addressed by using .claude/scripts/dso shim.
- **Title pattern not configurable** (medium, config): Projects with different conventions get persistent false positives. Addressed by adding dso-config.conf key.
- **Create-bug skill may bypass CLI_user tag logic** (medium, config): The skill may not distinguish invocation contexts. Addressed by not adding CLI_user by default — only the calling agent knows the context.
- **Unicode lookalike arrows trigger false positives** (low, runtime): Addressed by auto-converting unicode arrow to ASCII on create/edit.

## Research Findings

Discovery session (captured in epic comments) established: structured title format using Component-Behavior pattern, 0-4 priority rubric tied to blocker/workaround criteria, Zero Inference rule prohibiting root cause speculation and hindsight bias, chronological rationalization as a black-box recorder, scenario-specific rules for user-flagged/sub-agent/nitpick bugs, and gating criteria for low-value ticket prevention. Web research confirmed Jira supports UTF-8 in summary fields when database is configured for UTF-8 (default for Jira Cloud), but misconfigured instances may mangle unicode — use ASCII -> as canonical format.
